name: Ascent to Pla de Busa - Circular from Solsona
name_original: Subida al Pla de Busa - Circular desde Solsona
region_intro: Road cycling around Solsona, situated at 664 meters elevation within the Catalan Central Depression and at the foot of the Pre-Pyrenees, offers diverse terrain for cyclists. The region features a landscape of forests, fields, and dry-stone walls, with routes extending into the Pre-Pyrenees. This area provides a mix of climbs, descents, and flatter sections, catering to various fitness levels. Natural features like the Ribera Salada riverside and the Llosa del Cavall Reservoir are integrated into many routes. Best road cycling routes around Solsona The most popular road cycling route is Surrounding the Madrona river - circular from Solsona to Rialb, a 34.6 miles (55.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views along the Madrona river. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Ascent to Pla de Busa - Circular from Solsona, a difficult 45.9 miles (73.8 km) path. This route features substantial climbing towards Pla de Busa, providing a challenging experience. Local road cyclists also love the Solsona - Circular route via Olius, a 13.1 miles (21.1 km) trail leading through Olius, often completed in about 1 hour 5 minutes. This easy route passes by historical sites like the pre-Romanesque crypt in Olius. Road cycling around Solsona is defined by the Pre-Pyrenees foothills, the Solsonès Comarca's varied agricultural landscapes, and mountain passes like Coll de Jou and Coll de Port. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to challenging ascents. The routes in Solsona are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 1,300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Solsona's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Solsona?
A: Solsona offers a wide variety of road cycling routes, with over 60 different tours available. These routes cater to various fitness levels, from easy loops to challenging ascents into the Pre-Pyrenees.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for road cycling routes in Solsona?
A: The road cycling routes around Solsona span a range of difficulty levels. You'll find approximately 4 easy routes, 19 moderate routes, and 40 difficult routes, ensuring options for beginners and experienced cyclists alike.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Solsona?
A: Yes, Solsona has several easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938442705/solsona-circular-route-via-olius" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Solsona - Circular route via Olius&lt;/a&gt;, which is 21.1 km long and passes by historical sites like the pre-Romanesque crypt in Olius.

Q: What kind of landscapes and natural features can I expect to see while road cycling around Solsona?
A: Road cycling around Solsona offers diverse landscapes, from the Catalan Central Depression to the foothills of the Pre-Pyrenees. You'll encounter a mosaic of forests, fields, and dry-stone walls. Notable natural features integrated into many routes include the Ribera Salada riverside and the beautiful Llosa del Cavall Reservoir. Mountain passes like Coll de Jou and Coll de Port also offer dramatic scenery.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate historical and cultural landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938442705/solsona-circular-route-via-olius" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Solsona - Circular route via Olius&lt;/a&gt; passes by the pre-Romanesque crypt and modernist cemetery in Olius. Longer routes might also connect to significant sites like Cardona Castle. You can also find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4925019" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellvell de Olius&lt;/a&gt; offering panoramic views.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Solsona?
A: The best time to visit Solsona for road cycling is during spring and autumn. The weather during these seasons is generally most pleasant, offering comfortable temperatures for longer rides and enjoying the diverse landscapes without extreme heat or cold.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Solsona?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Solsona are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e943085943/surrounding-the-madrona-river-circular-from-solsona-to-rialb" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Surrounding the Madrona river - circular from Solsona to Rialb&lt;/a&gt; and the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937563467/ascent-to-pla-de-busa-circular-from-solsona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Pla de Busa - Circular from Solsona&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink along the road cycling routes in Solsona?
A: While specific cafes and pubs on every route are not detailed, Solsona itself and many of the smaller towns and villages you pass through will offer opportunities for refreshments. The region is known for its local heritage, and you can often find places to refuel. The Vilar de la Duquessa Bike Centre also offers services and sports breakfasts.

Q: What are the parking options for cyclists in Solsona?
A: Solsona, being a cycling-friendly area, generally offers parking options within the town. Many routes start directly from Solsona, making it convenient to park your vehicle and begin your ride. Specific parking facilities for cyclists are often available, especially near dedicated cycling centers.

Q: Is public transport available to access cycling routes in Solsona?
A: Solsona is accessible by public transport, primarily bus services, which can help you reach the starting points of various cycling routes. While direct public transport to every single trailhead might not be available, the town itself serves as a good hub for accessing the wider network of routes.

Q: What do other cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Solsona?
A: The road cycling experience in Solsona is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from the varied agricultural landscapes of the Solsonès Comarca to the challenging climbs of the Pre-Pyrenees, and the well-maintained routes that cater to different ability levels.

Q: Are there challenging routes for experienced road cyclists in Solsona?
A: Absolutely. Solsona is an excellent destination for experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937563467/ascent-to-pla-de-busa-circular-from-solsona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Pla de Busa - Circular from Solsona&lt;/a&gt;, with its substantial climbing, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17556401" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llosa del Cavall Reservoir – Solsona loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 100 km with significant elevation gain, provide demanding and rewarding experiences.

Q: Can I bring my dog on road cycling routes in Solsona?
A: While many natural areas in Solsona are dog-friendly for walking, road cycling routes typically follow paved roads and are primarily designed for human cyclists. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it is safely secured in a bike trailer or carrier, and always check local regulations regarding pets on specific roads or in natural parks you might pass through.
tips: This sanctuary dates from the 17th century and is located in the Solsonès region. Of strong Marian devotion, it was built following the apparition of the Virgin to two shepherds in the year 1458.

Between Solsona and Navès begins the hard gate of the Sierra de Busa with 14.4km long and 750m of positive difference in altitude. We will find maximum slopes of up to 17% and an average slope of 5.2% thanks to two long breaks that we will find halfway up the climb. It should be noted that km 11 is done at 9.6% and the last 400m have an average gradient of 11.7%. A demanding terrain that rewards you as soon as you reach the top where you can enjoy exceptional views of Solsonès and Bages, drawing Montserrat on the horizon. The Pla de Busa is located at 1372m.
